mechanism_narrative: A three-month trailing growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between 3-month RSAFS growth and subsequent 60-day SPX return is not significantly positive (Pearson r < 0.1) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months, it signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher stock prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of trigger instances, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate at or below 0.25% and year-over-year CPI inflation above 2% creates negative real interest rates, which push investors from cash and bonds into equities as an inflation hedge, driving prices up.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with moderate inflation
failure_condition: During periods satisfying the trigger, the proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where the federal funds rate is below 1%, rising industrial production signals real economic expansion without fear of immediate tightening, directly boosting corporate profits and equity valuations. In higher rate regimes, the effect reverses as it may trigger rate hikes.
failure_condition: In the accommodation regime, the rank correlation between 3-month INDPRO change and subsequent 60-day SPX return is less than 0.05 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts reading above 600,000 units for the first time after at least six months below that level signals a decisive housing market recovery, which leads to positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days due to increased construction and consumer wealth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 600 for first time in 6 months
failure_condition: In more than 50% of trigger occurrences, the 20-day SPX return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in the labor market (falling UNRATE) and consumer spending (rising RSAFS) creates a reinforcing cycle that boosts corporate earnings and equity prices more than either factor alone. Both series must trend positively over three months.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declining and RSAFS rising over 3 months
failure_condition: The combined signal does not improve the information coefficient over individual signals by at least 0.02 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate from below 0.25% to above 0.25% after a prolonged period at the zero lower bound signals the end of extreme accommodation, which negatively impacts equity valuations as discount rates rise and easy money recedes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% after being below 0.25% for at least 3 months
failure_condition: After trigger, the median 60-day SPX return is not negative, or more than 40% of instances have positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: The ratio of industrial production to retail sales (INDPRO/RSAFS) rising over one month signals that supply is outpacing demand, leading to inventory accumulation and potential production cuts, which depress corporate profits and stock prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between one-month change in INDPRO/RSAFS ratio and subsequent 5-day SPX return is not negative (Pearson r > -0.05) over a 3-year period.
